questions.text: Which is the most plentiful gas in the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up 78% of the air and is vital for plant life, although plants cannot take it directly from air.

questions.text: Which gas is released by green plants during photosynthesis and used by humans for breathing?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is the second most plentiful gas in the air (21%). Plants create it during photosynthesis.

questions.text: The greenhouse effect is primarily caused by which gas?
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide traps heat radiated from the earth, keeping the planet warm. Excessive CO2 leads to global warming.

questions.text: In which layer of the atmosphere do almost all weather phenomena like rainfall, fog and hailstorm occur?
questions.explanation: The Troposphere is the most important layer of the atmosphere, extending up to an average height of 13 km.

questions.text: The layer of the atmosphere that contains the ozone layer and is ideal for flying aeroplanes is:
questions.explanation: The Stratosphere is free from clouds and weather phenomena, and the ozone layer protects us from harmful sun rays.

questions.text: Meteorites burn up in this layer on entering from space:
questions.explanation: The Mesosphere is the third layer of the atmosphere and extends up to the height of 80 km.

questions.text: Which layer helps in radio transmission as radio waves are reflected back to earth?
questions.explanation: The Ionosphere is a part of the Thermosphere and facilitates long-distance communication.
Air pressure decreases as height increases; hence it is maximum at sea level.

questions.text: The horizontal movement of air from high pressure areas to low pressure areas is called:
questions.explanation: Winds are named after the direction from which they blow.

questions.text: A hot and dry local wind of the northern plains of India is called:
questions.explanation: Loo is a local wind that blows during the summer season.
